name: Weber Spirit II E-310 3-Burner Gas Grill
tag: Best Mid-Range Gas Grill for Long-Term Ownership

specs.Fuel Type: Liquid propane (natural gas model also available)
specs.Warranty: 10 years on burners, lid, and cook box

whatWorks: GS4 stainless steel burners light instantly via Infinity ignition every single time — no repeated clicking or manual lighting needed, a meaningful upgrade over ignitions found on cheaper grills.
Excellent heat distribution across the full cooking surface eliminates cold spots; burgers, steaks, and chicken kebabs cook evenly without rotating, and the grill reaches mid-500°F for a proper sear.
Flavorizer bars genuinely add smoky depth by vaporizing drippings on contact — particularly noticeable with fattier cuts like burgers and ribeye, where it noticeably edges out a plain gas grill.

Weber's build quality is a tier above most competitors at this price: heavy-gauge sheet metal, porcelain-enamel hood that resists rust, and cast-iron grates that outlast the thin stamped steel found on $400 grills — many owners report 8–10+ years of service with basic maintenance.
worthKnowing: No side burner — a feature that's increasingly common even on budget grills. If you rely on a side burner for sauces, corn, or side dishes, look at the Weber Spirit II E-330 or a Broil King Signet model instead.

Open undercarriage design means the propane tank is fully visible, which bothers buyers who want a clean patio aesthetic — the older Spirit E-310 (enclosed cabinet) or a cover solves this but adds cost.

Assembly takes about an hour for two people (49 steps) — longer than competitors with simpler designs. The Bilt app with 3D animated instructions helps significantly and is worth downloading before you start.

At $649 CAD on Amazon.ca, it costs more than similarly spec'd Broil King Signet models (which offer higher BTU output). The Weber wins on long-term durability and brand support, but the price premium is real.
verdict: The Weber Spirit II E-310 is the benchmark mid-range gas grill — not the flashiest or cheapest option, but the one most likely to still be running a decade from now. It earns its price through consistently even heat, a reliable ignition system, flavorizer bars that actually improve flavor, and Weber's 10-year burner warranty. Canadian buyers who want a reliable gas grill for regular family use and don't need a side burner will be very happy with this purchase.

realBuyers.quote: It's probably the most well-built piece of outdoor equipment I own — zero issues after four years, still lights first try every time, and the cast-iron grates look better than my neighbours' year-old cheap grills.
Assembly took my wife and me about 75 minutes with the Bilt app — instructions were genuinely clear. First cook the next day and the burgers were the best I've made at home.
Compared to my old Broil King the heat is more even and the ignition never fails. I do miss having a side burner for corn on the cob but it wasn't a deal-breaker.

metaDescription: Weber Spirit II E-310 review for Canadian buyers: even heat, reliable GS4 ignition, and 10-year warranty make it the benchmark mid-range gas grill at $649 CAD.
introText: The Weber Spirit II E-310 is a three-burner propane gas grill built around Weber's GS4 system — Infinity electronic ignition, stainless steel burners, flavorizer bars, and porcelain-enameled cast-iron grates. At $649 CAD on Amazon.ca, it sits at the upper end of the mid-range gas grill category but backs up its price with a 10-year burner warranty and a reputation for outlasting nearly everything in its class.
